records.ety: [F. prtexte, L. praetextum, fr. praetextus, p. p. of praetexere to weave before, allege as an excuse; prae before + texere to weave. See Text.]

defs: Ostensible reason or motive assigned or assumed as a color or cover for the real reason or motive; pretense; disguise.
quotes: They suck the blood of those they depend on, under a pretext of service and kindness.
With how much or how little pretext of reason.
